title: Pilosella tephroglauca
taxonomy: Pilosella tephroglauca is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The species is part of the Pilosella genus, which is a group of plants that were previously classified as Hieracium but reclassified due to genetic evidence. This reclassification was based on studies of the plants' chromosome numbers and genetic makeup. The Pilosella genus is a complex group with many species, and classification can sometimes be disputed.

This plant is valued for its traditional use in herbal medicine, where it has been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, offering relief from digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
